behavior_summary: U8P_GHOUL::look is a simple U8P_GHOUL::look/examine handler for the Ghoul entity. When the player examines the Ghoul, the script issues a single player-facing bark containing the text "ghoul " and then suspends/ends the U8P_GHOUL::look process.
There is no branching, menu interaction, global-flag manipulation, or external API calls in this script.
The presence of a suspend/terminate node indicates the handler yields or finishes immediately after emitting the bark.
notable_control_flow: Linear one-shot flow: entry -> emit bark "ghoul " -> suspend/terminate.
No conditional branches, loops, or external calls are present in the U8P_GHOUL::look handler.
open_questions: Does the engine interpret the 'suspend' as an immediate end-of-process or as a yield that can be resumed later? (inferred behavior only)
Is the trailing space in the bark text "ghoul " intentional (formatting/concatenation placeholder) or an artifact of extraction?
Are there context-sensitive variants (e.g., different barks under certain global flags) implemented elsewhere that call or override U8P_GHOUL::look?

main_function.name: U8P_GHOUL::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description / examine response for the Ghoul NPC: display a single bark and then end or suspend the U8P_GHOUL::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player uses the 'U8P_GHOUL::look' or examine action on the Ghoul NPC (inferred from classification 'U8P_GHOUL::look' and process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_GHOUL::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that emits a one-line bark for the Ghoul and ends the process.
